pattern: The chef is only ever free at one running moment in time, so each customer's start time is just the later of their own arrival and that single running clock -- no scheduling structure beyond a running maximum is needed.
hints: The chef handles orders strictly in the order customers arrive, so you never need to reorder anything -- just walk the list once.
Keep a single 'chef free at' timestamp. Each customer's cooking actually starts at max(their arrival, that timestamp), and finishes `time` minutes later.
A customer's wait is simply (their finish time) minus (their arrival time); accumulate that finish time as the new 'chef free at' value and average the waits at the end.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, three customers with idle gaps
statement example, four customers with a long queue building up
several customers arrive at the exact same instant and must queue
arrivals are spaced far enough apart that the chef is always idle beforehand
minimum and maximum prep/arrival values from the constraints

stress.description: large customer list with non-decreasing arrivals within the value bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    arrivals = sorted(rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n))
    customers = [[a, rng.randint(1, 10000)] for a in arrivals]
    return [customers]
